channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Domestic rubbish bag on pavement, 25th April
item.description:
Please check and remove Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 98, Mickleham Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London

item.description:
Bins down Victoria Park road have overflowed

item.title: Burnt out bin!, 25th April
item.description:
Please remove burnt out bin, it is a health hazard!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Felixstowe Road, Abbey Wood

item.title: Lack of fox-proof bin, 25th April
item.description:
Open bins are routinely rummaged by foxes. They spread rubbish across the park. The bin needs replacing with a covered bin.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maitland Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Someone dumping there rubbish bags, 25th April
item.description:
Someone dumping black bags of rubbish and now been ripped open and rubbish all over the grass this keeps happening what do we pay for

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Palm Avenue, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car abandoned at the top of The Chenies in middle of road, 25th April
item.description:
Road hazard - abandoned car

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chenies, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter on communal area, 25th April
item.description:
I was informed that this issue has now been “fixed.” However, I would like to clarify what actions have been taken, as there has been no noticeable improvement. The area remains in a poor state and does not appear to be receiving the more frequent cleaning that was outlined in the regulations. Could you please confirm: What specific measures have been implemented to address this issue? Whether the request has been passed to another department or contractor? When we can expect to see a consistent and noticeable improvement in cleaning standards? I would appreciate a prompt update, as the current condition of the communal area is still unsatisfactory.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glengall Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Landing and stair washing, 25th April
item.description:
I was informed that this issue has now been “fixed.” However, I would like to clarify what actions have been taken, as there has been no noticeable improvement. The area remains in a poor state and does not appear to be receiving the more frequent cleaning that was outlined in the regulations. Could you please confirm: What specific measures have been implemented to address this issue? Whether the request has been passed to another department or contractor? When we can expect to see a consistent and noticeable improvement in cleaning standards? I would appreciate a prompt update, as the current condition of the communal area is still unsatisfactory.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glengall Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 25th April
item.description:
People use this old closed shop yard to damp anything the want. Thewhole area is filthy.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Endwell Road, Brockley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish and gas canisters, 25th April
item.description:
All over the road and pavement, this happens all the time here, why not put up cameras?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Norfolk Place, East Wickham, Greater London

item.title: Broken armchair, 25th April
item.description:
Broken armchair dumped on street, been sat there for about two weeks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Evering Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Chippy and dentist shutters, 25th April
item.description:
Please clean soonest. Lets the rest of the parade of businesses on both sides down.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 47, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin Bags Left Out Overnight, Foxes Invited, Street Converted Into Landfill. (Manor Road, EN2 0QN), 25th April
item.description:
Rubbish left out overnight, as apparently required/encouraged by the current system. By morning it has been very efficiently distributed across Manor Road. A real triumph of modern waste management. The result is food waste, torn bags, wrappers and general rubbish spread all over the street, creating a mess, attracting animals and making the area look neglected. Could this please be cleaned up urgently, and could the council review the wisdom of any policy that results in residents having to leave loose rubbish out overnight for wildlife to redecorate the road?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Manor Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shop front vandalism, 25th April
item.description:
Please get shop to clean.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead bird on foot path, 25th April
item.description:
A dead bird has been found on the foot path.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lonsdale Drive, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping bus stop X (EN2), 25th April
item.description:
Every day some resident fly tips rubbish at the bins beside Bus Stop X on Chase Side (EN2 0QN). Emphasis on every day. Perhaps the council could investigate though prob not a great photo opportunity for Ergin!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chase Side, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Obscene/Nazi images, 25th April
item.description:
Clean immediately and investigate.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 79c, Goodrich Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
